perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in force for five (5) years from the Effective Date, except to the extent this Agreement is superseded by stipulations of the Contemplated Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in force for one (1) year from the Effective Date, except to the extent this Agreement is superseded by stipulations of the Contemplated Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term of the agreement is reduced from five years to one year, creating an in-text contradiction with section VII, clause 3, which states that the rights and obligations remain in effect for five years from the disclosure date. This creates uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights and obligations of each Party with respect to all Confidential Information of the other Party that is received under this Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the agreement's duration from five years to one year. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that confidentiality obligations remain in effect for five years from the disclosure date. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the different sections of the contract now provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed herein shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party shall obtain no right thereto of any kind by reason of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information disclosed herein shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party shall have a perpetual, royalty-free license to use such information for internal research purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Receiving Party gains no rights to the Confidential Information. The modification grants a perpetual, royalty-free license for internal research, creating a contradiction. The in-text contradiction introduces a legal ambiguity about the Disclosing Party's rights, as it now conflicts with the Receiving Party's new rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed herein shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party, except that the Receiving Party shall have a perpetual, royalty-free license to use such information for internal research purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Receiving Party obtains no rights to the Confidential Information. The changed text grants the Receiving Party a perpetual, royalty-free license to use the information for internal research purposes. This creates a direct and undeniable contradiction because granting a license inherently provides a right to the Receiving Party that the original text denies. The contradicted text is a replica of the change itself, further emphasizing the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any amendment to this Agreement shall be agreed in writing by both Parties and shall refer to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Any amendment to this Agreement shall be agreed to verbally by both Parties and will not need to refer to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section X states that amendments must be agreed in writing, while the changed text says amendments can be agreed to verbally. The in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about the validity and enforcement of amendments to the agreement, due to conflicting requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any amendment to this Agreement shall be agreed to verbally by both Parties and will not need to refer to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments to the agreement must be agreed upon in writing. The changed text states that amendments can be agreed to verbally and will not need to refer to the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction, as the two clauses specify conflicting requirements for how amendments can be made, and the explanation states that this creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall disclose of any similar agreements explicit or otherwise, for similar purpose/application with in its own organization, or any other third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to disclose any similar agreements explicit or otherwise, for similar purpose/application within its own organization, or any other third party.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, Section XIII, clause 3 obligates the Receiving Party to disclose any similar agreements. By stating the Receiving Party is not required to disclose any similar agreements it is creating an in-text contradiction to the text before hand.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party is not required to disclose any similar agreements explicit or otherwise, for similar purpose/application within its own organization, or any other third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to disclose similar agreements. The changed text states that the Receiving Party is not required to disclose similar agreements, which directly contradicts the original requirement. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as described by the explanation and fulfills the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' by providing conflicting obligations. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon request from the Disclosing Party or upon the conclusion of the Purpose or expiry of this Agreement, the Receiving Party, at its own cost, will return or procure the return, promptly and in any event within 14 days of receipt of such request, of each and every copy of Confidential Information given by the Disclosing Party, and satisfy the Disclosing Party that it no longer holds any further Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon request from the Disclosing Party or upon the conclusion of the Purpose or expiry of this Agreement, the Receiving Party, at its own cost, will return or procure the return, promptly and in any event within 30 days of receipt of such request, of each and every copy of Confidential Information given by the Disclosing Party, and satisfy the Disclosing Party that it no longer holds any further Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text dictates that the Receiving Party should return the Confidential Information within 14 days, where as the the modification dictates that the Receiving Party return the Confidental Information within 30 days, causing a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.location: V. Copying and Return of Furnished Instruments: 3
perturbation.contradicted_location: V. Copying and Return of Furnished Instruments: 3
perturbation.contradicted_text: At any time upon request from the Disclosing Party or upon the conclusion of the Purpose or expiry of this Agreement, the Receiving Party, at its own cost, will return or procure the return, promptly and in any event within 30 days of receipt of such request, of each and every copy of Confidential Information given by the Disclosing Party, and satisfy the Disclosing Party that it no longer holds any further Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 14-day return window for confidential information, but the changed text extends this to 30 days. The contradicted text also states 30 days as the return window. This creates a direct inconsistency regarding the timeframe within which the Receiving Party must return the Confidential Information, since the original text states 14 days, but the changed text and contradicted text states 30 days. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where conflicting deadlines are introduced.
